title: athlete — declared entitlements
description: Three entitlement keys. `athlete_slot` caps active-athlete headcount (aligns with user_slot per hierarchy.md §7 — most tenants budget athletes + users together). `athlete_medical_tracking` is an OPT-IN boolean gating the medical cluster (regulated_health-tier data). `athlete_consent_workflow` gates the full recordable consent workflow (Enterprise only — tracks per-purpose expiry + revocation for GDPR Art. 8 audit-ready evidence).

entitlements.description: Active + paused athlete headcount cap. Small: 15 (micro-academy / studio profile). Medium: 250 (typical academy). Enterprise: unlimited (null cap). Consumed on Athlete.creating + on status archived → active (restore). Released on status → archived + on hard-delete. Graduated and withdrawn athletes ALSO release the slot at the transition — graduating an athlete lets a tenant free up capacity for a new intake without paying to archive them (soft-terminal state preserves alumni relations for free). Matches user_slot at each tier per hierarchy.md §7 rationale (a tenant that pays for 15 users typically wants 15 athletes; a Medium tenant with 250 users likely runs 250 athletes).
enforcedBy: AthleteObserver.updating (status → archived / graduated / withdrawn) releases 1 unit
AthleteObserver.deleted (soft-delete) is a no-op (release already fired on the transition)
TenantSubscription downgrade triggers a warning via the reconciler but does NOT auto-archive over-quota rows — HR/admin must choose which athletes to archive

entitlements.description: Opt-in gate for the medical_conditions / medical_allergies / medical_medications / medical_notes columns. OFF on Small tier by default (Small tenants without HR staff shouldn't accidentally collect regulated_health data); ON on Medium + Enterprise. When off, columns MUST be NULL — observer refuses writes with ATHLETE_MEDICAL_TRACKING_NOT_ENABLED (402). When on, columns become writable but still require `athlete.manage.medical` permission on every write path (permission separate from entitlement — the gate says the FEATURE is available; the permission says WHO can use it).
enforcedBy: AthleteObserver.creating refuses medical_* writes when gate is off
AthleteObserver.updating refuses medical_* writes when gate is off
athlete.enforce_medical_permission middleware returns 402 on the /medical sub-resource when the gate is off (before even checking the caller's permission — the feature isn't available)
SDUI medical section hidden when features array does not include 'athlete.medical_tracking'
entitlements.compliance_note: This gate is a data-minimisation lever. Turning it ON is a compliance-relevant tenant decision — the ops team logs the toggle in the compliance audit trail. Small tenants who need medical tracking on a small athlete count can pay for a one-off Medium upgrade rather than the whole platform expanding its regulated-health footprint by default.

entitlements.description: Enterprise-only. Enables the full recordable consent workflow: recordable events (AthleteConsentRecorded / AthleteConsentRevoked), 12-month re-consent reminder cadence (via ReconcileAthleteConsentsJob), per-purpose retention log, and the /athletes/{athlete}/consents sub-resource. When off (Small + Medium default), the consent snapshot on the Athlete row is a bare three-boolean set with `consent_recorded_at` + `consent_recorded_by_user_id` — no expiry, no reminders, no per-flag history. When on, the workflow adds audit-ready evidence for GDPR Art. 8 + safeguarding regulator inquiries.
enforcedBy: AthleteConsentGate::isWorkflowEnabled(tenant) returns false for Small + Medium tiers → the /consents sub-resource returns 402 ATHLETE_CONSENT_WORKFLOW_NOT_ENABLED
ReconcileAthleteConsentsJob skips tenants without the entitlement
SDUI consent-workflow surface (add/revoke consent modals, expiry chip on athlete profile) hidden when features array does not include 'athlete.consent_workflow'
